sections.paragraph:
φέρω [Skt. bhárati ‘bear’, cp. Lat. fero ‘bear’]

—1. ‘move an entity from one position to another’

—a. through physical transport bring Mt 14:11, 18; Mk 1:32; Lk 24:1; J 2:8; 4:33; 19:39; Ac 4:34, 37; 2 Ti 4:13. In Lk 23:26 two aspects are apparent: support of a burden and transport.

—b. through guidance conduct, lead, direct Mk 15:22; J 20:27 (a finger); 21:18; Ac 14:13; 27:15; Hb 6:1; 1 Pt 1:13; 2 Pt 1:21b; Rv 21:24. Of a strong wind put into motion Ac 2:2. Of bad weather 27:15, 17. In imagery, of a gate positioned toward the city 12:10.

—2. ‘direct someth. that is of a cognitive nature’, bear, bring, direct: judicial charge, complaint J 18:29; Ac 25:18; message 2 Pt 1:17f, 21a; 2:11; 2 J 10;establish Hb 9:16.

—3. ‘support what is burdensome’, bear: the universe in all its aspects Hb 1:3; anything requiring patience or endurance Ro 9:22; Hb 12:20; 13:13.

—4. ‘bring about a yield’, bear, produce Mt 7:18 v.l.; Mk 4:8; J 15:2 and freq. in the ch.
